<task id="_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml" name="Execute Workflow" internal="true">
  <objective>Execute given workflow by loading its configuration, following instructions, and producing output</objective>

  <llm critical="true">
    <mandate>Always read COMPLETE files - NEVER use offset/limit when reading any workflow related files</mandate>
    <mandate>Instructions are MANDATORY - either as file path, steps or embedded list in YAML, XML or markdown</mandate>
    <mandate>Execute ALL steps in instructions IN EXACT ORDER</mandate>
    <mandate>Save to template output file after EVERY "template-output" tag</mandate>
    <mandate>NEVER skip a step - YOU are responsible for every steps execution without fail or excuse</mandate>
  </llm>

  <WORKFLOW-RULES critical="true">
    <rule n="1">Steps execute in exact numerical order (1, 2, 3...)</rule>
    <rule n="2">Optional steps: Ask user unless #yolo mode active</rule>
    <rule n="3">Template-output tags: Save content, discuss with the user the section completed, and NEVER proceed until the users indicates
      to proceed (unless YOLO mode has been activated)</rule>
  </WORKFLOW-RULES>

  <flow>
    <step n="1" title="Load and Initialize Workflow">
      <substep n="1a" title="Load Configuration and Resolve Variables">
        <action>Read workflow.yaml from provided path</action>
        <mandate>Load config_source (REQUIRED for all modules)</mandate>
        <phase n="1">Load external config from config_source path</phase>
        <phase n="2">Resolve all {config_source}: references with values from config</phase>
        <phase n="3">Resolve system variables (date:system-generated) and paths ({project-root}, {installed_path})</phase>
        <phase n="4">Ask user for input of any variables that are still unknown</phase>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1b" title="Load Required Components">
        <mandate>Instructions: Read COMPLETE file from path OR embedded list (REQUIRED)</mandate>
        <check>If template path → Read COMPLETE template file</check>
        <check>If validation path → Note path for later loading when needed</check>
        <check>If template: false → Mark as action-workflow (else template-workflow)</check>
        <note>Data files (csv, json) → Store paths only, load on-demand when instructions reference them</note>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1c" title="Initialize Output" if="template-workflow">
        <action>Resolve default_output_file path with all variables and {{date}}</action>
        <action>Create output directory if doesn't exist</action>
        <action>If template-workflow → Write template to output file with placeholders</action>
        <action>If action-workflow → Skip file creation</action>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="2" title="Process Each Instruction Step in Order">
      <iterate>For each step in instructions:</iterate>

      <substep n="2a" title="Handle Step Attributes">
        <check>If optional="true" and NOT #yolo → Ask user to include</check>
        <check>If if="condition" → Evaluate condition</check>
        <check>If for-each="item" → Repeat step for each item</check>
        <check>If repeat="n" → Repeat step n times</check>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2b" title="Execute Step Content">
        <action>Process step instructions (markdown or XML tags)</action>
        <action>Replace {{variables}} with values (ask user if unknown)</action>
        <execute-tags>
          <tag>action xml tag → Perform the action</tag>
          <tag>check if="condition" xml tag → Conditional block wrapping actions (requires closing </check>)</tag>
          <tag>ask xml tag → Prompt user and WAIT for response</tag>
          <tag>invoke-workflow xml tag → Execute another workflow with given inputs and the workflow.xml runner</tag>
          <tag>invoke-task xml tag → Execute specified task</tag>
          <tag>invoke-protocol name="protocol_name" xml tag → Execute reusable protocol from protocols section</tag>
          <tag>goto step="x" → Jump to specified step</tag>
        </execute-tags>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2c" title="Handle template-output Tags">
        <if tag="template-output">
          <mandate>Generate content for this section</mandate>
          <mandate>Save to file (Write first time, Edit subsequent)</mandate>
          <action>Display generated content</action>
          <ask> [a] Advanced Elicitation, [c] Continue, [p] Party-Mode, [y] YOLO the rest of this document only. WAIT for response. <if
              response="a">
              <action>Start the advanced elicitation workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/advanced-elicitation/workflow.xml</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="c">
              <action>Continue to next step</action>
            </if>
            <if response="p">
              <action>Start the party-mode workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/party-mode/workflow.md</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="y">
              <action>Enter #yolo mode for the rest of the workflow</action>
            </if>
          </ask>
        </if>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2d" title="Step Completion">
        <check>If no special tags and NOT #yolo:</check>
        <ask>Continue to next step? (y/n/edit)</ask>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="3" title="Completion">
      <check>Confirm document saved to output path</check>
      <action>Report workflow completion</action>
    </step>
  </flow>

  <execution-modes>
    <mode name="normal">Full user interaction and confirmation of EVERY step at EVERY template output - NO EXCEPTIONS except yolo MODE</mode>
    <mode name="yolo">Skip all confirmations and elicitation, minimize prompts and try to produce all of the workflow automatically by
      simulating the remaining discussions with an simulated expert user</mode>
  </execution-modes>

  <supported-tags desc="Instructions can use these tags">
    <structural>
      <tag>step n="X" goal="..." - Define step with number and goal</tag>
      <tag>optional="true" - Step can be skipped</tag>
      <tag>if="condition" - Conditional execution</tag>
      <tag>for-each="collection" - Iterate over items</tag>
      <tag>repeat="n" - Repeat n times</tag>
    </structural>
    <execution>
      <tag>action - Required action to perform</tag>
      <tag>action if="condition" - Single conditional action (inline, no closing tag needed)</tag>
      <tag>check if="condition">...</check> - Conditional block wrapping multiple items (closing tag required)</tag>
      <tag>ask - Get user input (ALWAYS wait for response before continuing)</tag>
      <tag>goto - Jump to another step</tag>
      <tag>invoke-workflow - Call another workflow</tag>
      <tag>invoke-task - Call a task</tag>
      <tag>invoke-protocol - Execute a reusable protocol (e.g., discover_inputs)</tag>
    </execution>
    <output>
      <tag>template-output - Save content checkpoint</tag>
      <tag>critical - Cannot be skipped</tag>
      <tag>example - Show example output</tag>
    </output>
  </supported-tags>

  <protocols desc="Reusable workflow protocols that can be invoked via invoke-protocol tag">
    <protocol name="discover_inputs" desc="Smart file discovery and loading based on input_file_patterns">
      <objective>Intelligently load project files (whole or sharded) based on workflow's input_file_patterns configuration</objective>

      <critical>Only execute if workflow.yaml contains input_file_patterns section</critical>

      <flow>
        <step n="1" title="Parse Input File Patterns">
          <action>Read input_file_patterns from loaded workflow.yaml</action>
          <action>For each pattern group (prd, architecture, epics, etc.), note the load_strategy if present</action>
        </step>

        <step n="2" title="Load Files Using Smart Strategies">
          <iterate>For each pattern in input_file_patterns:</iterate>

          <substep n="2a" title="Try Sharded Documents First">
            <check if="sharded pattern exists">
              <action>Determine load_strategy from pattern config (defaults to FULL_LOAD if not specified)</action>

              <strategy name="FULL_LOAD">
                <desc>Load ALL files in sharded directory - used for PRD, Architecture, UX, brownfield docs</desc>
                <action>Use glob pattern to find ALL .md files (e.g., "{output_folder}/*architecture*/*.md")</action>
                <action>Load EVERY matching file completely</action>
                <action>Concatenate content in logical order (index.md first if exists, then alphabetical)</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="SELECTIVE_LOAD">
                <desc>Load specific shard using template variable - example: used for epics with {{epic_num}}</desc>
                <action>Check for template variables in sharded_single pattern (e.g., {{epic_num}})</action>
                <action>If variable undefined, ask user for value OR infer from context</action>
                <action>Resolve template to specific file path</action>
                <action>Load that specific file</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="INDEX_GUIDED">
                <desc>Load index.md, analyze structure and description of each doc in the index, then intelligently load relevant docs</desc>
                <mandate>DO NOT BE LAZY - use best judgment to load documents that might have relevant information, even if only a 5% chance</mandate>
                <action>Load index.md from sharded directory</action>
                <action>Parse table of contents, links, section headers</action>
                <action>Analyze workflow's purpose and objective</action>
                <action>Identify which linked/referenced documents are likely relevant</action>
                <example>If workflow is about authentication and index shows "Auth Overview", "Payment Setup", "Deployment" → Load auth
                  docs, consider deployment docs, skip payment</example>
                <action>Load all identified relevant documents</action>
                <action>Store combined content in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
                <note>When in doubt, LOAD IT - context is valuable, being thorough is better than missing critical info</note>
              </strategy>

# Review Story Workflow
name: code-review
description: 'Perform adversarial code review finding specific issues. Use when the user says "run code review" or "review this code"'

# Critical variables from config
config_source: "{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/config.yaml"
user_name: "{config_source}:user_name"
communication_language: "{config_source}:communication_language"
user_skill_level: "{config_source}:user_skill_level"
document_output_language: "{config_source}:document_output_language"
date: system-generated
planning_artifacts: "{config_source}:planning_artifacts"
implementation_artifacts: "{config_source}:implementation_artifacts"
sprint_status: "{implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml"

# Workflow components
installed_path: "{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/workflows/4-implementation/code-review"
instructions: "{installed_path}/instructions.xml"
validation: "{installed_path}/checklist.md"
template: false

project_context: "**/project-context.md"

# Smart input file references - handles both whole docs and sharded docs
# Priority: Whole document first, then sharded version
# Strategy: SELECTIVE LOAD - only load the specific epic needed for this story review
input_file_patterns:
  architecture:
    description: "System architecture for review context"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*architecture*.md"
    sharded: "{planning_artifacts}/*architecture*/*.md"
    load_strategy: "FULL_LOAD"
  ux_design:
    description: "UX design specification (if UI review)"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*ux*.md"
    sharded: "{planning_artifacts}/*ux*/*.md"
    load_strategy: "FULL_LOAD"
  epics:
    description: "Epic containing story being reviewed"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*.md"
    sharded_index: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*/index.md"
    sharded_single: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*/epic-{{epic_num}}.md"
    load_strategy: "SELECTIVE_LOAD"

      2. **Create action items** - Add to story Tasks/Subtasks for later
      3. **Show me details** - Deep dive into specific issues

      Choose [1], [2], or specify which issue to examine:</ask>

    <check if="user chooses 1">
      <action>Fix all HIGH and MEDIUM issues in the code</action>
      <action>Add/update tests as needed</action>
      <action>Update File List in story if files changed</action>
      <action>Update story Dev Agent Record with fixes applied</action>
      <action>Set {{fixed_count}} = number of HIGH and MEDIUM issues fixed</action>
      <action>Set {{action_count}} = 0</action>
    </check>

    <check if="user chooses 2">
      <action>Add "Review Follow-ups (AI)" subsection to Tasks/Subtasks</action>
      <action>For each issue: `- [ ] [AI-Review][Severity] Description [file:line]`</action>
      <action>Set {{action_count}} = number of action items created</action>
      <action>Set {{fixed_count}} = 0</action>
    </check>

    <check if="user chooses 3">
      <action>Show detailed explanation with code examples</action>
      <action>Return to fix decision</action>
    </check>
  </step>

  <step n="5" goal="Update story status and sync sprint tracking">
    <!-- Determine new status based on review outcome -->
    <check if="all HIGH and MEDIUM issues fixed AND all ACs implemented">
      <action>Set {{new_status}} = "done"</action>
      <action>Update story Status field to "done"</action>
    </check>
    <check if="HIGH or MEDIUM issues remain OR ACs not fully implemented">
      <action>Set {{new_status}} = "in-progress"</action>
      <action>Update story Status field to "in-progress"</action>
    </check>
    <action>Save story file</action>

    <!-- Determine sprint tracking status -->
    <check if="{sprint_status} file exists">
      <action>Set {{current_sprint_status}} = "enabled"</action>
    </check>
    <check if="{sprint_status} file does NOT exist">
      <action>Set {{current_sprint_status}} = "no-sprint-tracking"</action>
    </check>

    <!-- Sync sprint-status.yaml when story status changes (only if sprint tracking enabled) -->
    <check if="{{current_sprint_status}} != 'no-sprint-tracking'">
      <action>Load the FULL file: {sprint_status}</action>
      <action>Find development_status key matching {{story_key}}</action>

      <check if="{{new_status}} == 'done'">
        <action>Update development_status[{{story_key}}] = "done"</action>
        <action>Save file, preserving ALL comments and structure</action>
        <output>✅ Sprint status synced: {{story_key}} → done</output>
      </check>

      <check if="{{new_status}} == 'in-progress'">
        <action>Update development_status[{{story_key}}] = "in-progress"</action>
        <action>Save file, preserving ALL comments and structure</action>
        <output>🔄 Sprint status synced: {{story_key}} → in-progress</output>
      </check>

      <check if="story key not found in sprint status">
        <output>⚠️ Story file updated, but sprint-status sync failed: {{story_key}} not found in sprint-status.yaml</output>
      </check>
    </check>

    <check if="{{current_sprint_status}} == 'no-sprint-tracking'">
      <output>ℹ️ Story status updated (no sprint tracking configured)</output>
    </check>

    <output>**✅ Review Complete!**

      **Story Status:** {{new_status}}
      **Issues Fixed:** {{fixed_count}}
      **Action Items Created:** {{action_count}}

      {{#if new_status == "done"}}Code review complete!{{else}}Address the action items and continue development.{{/if}}
    </output>
  </step>

1→# Story 7.1: Configure Alert Routing to Teams and Email
     2→
     3→Status: review
     4→
     5→<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
     6→
     7→## Story
     8→
     9→As a Platform Admin (Juliano),
    10→I want Robusta configured to send enriched alerts to Teams and Email based on severity levels,
    11→so that the right people are notified of issues at the right urgency.
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→
    15→1. **Given** Robusta is deployed with playbooks and sinks configured
    16→   **When** a job fails after all 3 retries
    17→   **Then** an enriched alert (with pod logs, resource usage, suggested fix) is sent to Teams
    18→
    19→2. **Given** severity-based routing is configured
    20→   **When** a Critical severity alert fires
    21→   **Then** it is routed to Teams OPS channel + Email to on-call (immediate response)
    22→
    23→3. **Given** severity-based routing is configured
    24→   **When** a High severity alert fires
    25→   **Then** it is routed to Teams OPS channel + Email (business hours response)
    26→
    27→4. **Given** severity-based routing is configured
    28→   **When** a Medium severity alert fires
    29→   **Then** it is routed to Email only (next business day review)
    30→
    31→5. **Given** severity-based routing is configured
    32→   **When** a Low severity alert fires
    33→   **Then** it appears in Grafana dashboard only (weekly review)
    34→
    35→6. **Given** an alert condition triggers
    36→   **When** Robusta evaluates the condition
    37→   **Then** the alert is delivered within 60 seconds of the condition trigger (NFR24)
    38→
    39→7. **Given** a job has failed after all 3 retries
    40→   **When** the final retry failure is detected
    41→   **Then** an alert is sent within 2 minutes of that final failure (NFR9)
    42→
    43→8. **Given** the alerting pipeline is operational
    44→   **When** measured over a reporting period
    45→   **Then** mean failure detection time is < 5 minutes across all alert types (NFR10)
    46→
    47→## Tasks / Subtasks
    48→
    49→- [x] Task 1: Configure Robusta Teams sink with Power Automate webhook (AC: #1, #2, #3)
    50→  - [x] 1.1: Create Power Automate workflow webhook in Teams OPS channel
    51→  - [x] 1.2: Add `ms_teams_sink` configuration in Robusta Helm values with scope filters
    52→  - [x] 1.3: Configure Critical severity → Teams OPS channel routing
    53→  - [x] 1.4: Configure High severity → Teams OPS channel routing
    54→
    55→- [x] Task 2: Configure Robusta Email (mail) sink (AC: #2, #3, #4)
    56→  - [x] 2.1: Configure SMTP relay or corporate mail gateway credentials
    57→  - [x] 2.2: Add `mail_sink` for Critical severity → on-call email
    58→  - [x] 2.3: Add `mail_sink` for High severity → ops team email
    59→  - [x] 2.4: Add `mail_sink` for Medium severity → ops team email (sole channel)
    60→
    61→- [x] Task 3: Configure Low severity → Dashboard-only routing (AC: #5)
    62→  - [x] 3.1: Ensure Low/Info severity alerts are excluded from Teams and Email sinks via `scope.exclude`
    63→  - [x] 3.2: Verify Grafana AlertManager datasource displays low-severity alerts natively
    64→
    65→- [x] Task 4: Create CronJob failure playbooks with enrichment (AC: #1, #6, #7)
    66→  - [x] 4.1: Add `on_job_failure` playbook with `job_info_enricher`, `job_events_enricher`, `job_pod_enricher`
    67→  - [x] 4.2: Add `on_pod_crash_loop` playbook with `logs_enricher` and `pod_events_enricher`
    68→  - [x] 4.3: Add `on_pod_oom_killed` playbook with `pod_graph_enricher` (Memory) and `logs_enricher`
    69→  - [x] 4.4: Route job failure playbooks to both Teams and Email sinks
    70→
    71→- [x] Task 5: Create PrometheusRule for CronJob failure detection (AC: #7, #8)
    72→  - [x] 5.1: Create `PrometheusRule` CR for `CronJobFailed` alert (kube_job_status_failed > 0)
    73→  - [x] 5.2: Create `PrometheusRule` CR for `CronJobRetryExhausted` alert (backoffLimit reached)
    74→  - [x] 5.3: Wire `on_prometheus_alert` playbook to enrich CronJob-specific alerts
    75→
    76→- [x] Task 6: Configure AlertManager → Robusta integration (AC: #6, #8)
    77→  - [x] 6.1: Add Robusta webhook receiver in AlertManager config (embedded in kube-prometheus-stack)
    78→  - [x] 6.2: Set `group_wait: 1s`, `group_interval: 1s` for fast alert delivery
    79→  - [x] 6.3: Enable `send_resolved: true` for resolved event tracking
    80→
    81→- [x] Task 7: Validate end-to-end alert delivery (AC: all)
    82→  - [x] 7.1: Deploy a test CronJob that intentionally fails
    83→  - [x] 7.2: Verify Teams notification arrives within 60 seconds
    84→  - [x] 7.3: Verify Email notification arrives within 60 seconds
    85→  - [x] 7.4: Verify enrichment includes pod logs, resource usage, suggested fix
    86→  - [x] 7.5: Verify Low severity alerts appear only in Grafana dashboard
    87→
    88→## Dev Notes
    89→
    90→### Critical Dependency: Epic 5 (Monitoring Stack)
    91→
    92→**This story REQUIRES the monitoring stack from Epic 5 (Story 5.1) to be deployed first.** Specifically:
    93→- Robusta with embedded kube-prometheus-stack (Prometheus + Grafana + AlertManager)
    94→- Prometheus scraping CronJob pod metrics
    95→- Grafana with AlertManager datasource configured
    96→
    97→If Epic 5 is not yet implemented, the developer must either:
    98→1. Implement Story 5.1 first (deploy LGTM stack), OR
    99→2. Deploy a minimal Robusta installation sufficient for alerting (without full LGTM backends)
   100→
   101→**Recommended approach:** Deploy Robusta with `enablePrometheusStack: true` as a self-contained unit. The full LGTM backends (Mimir, Loki, Tempo) can be added later in Story 5.1 without disrupting the alerting pipeline.
   102→
   103→### Architecture Patterns and Constraints
   104→
   105→**Robusta Version:** Use Robusta Helm chart v0.33.0+ (latest stable as of March 2026)
   106→- Chart repo: `https://robusta-charts.storage.googleapis.com`
   107→- **BREAKING CHANGE in v0.32.0:** Holmes AI dependency updated to 0.19.0 with breaking changes. If using Holmes, review changelog before deploying.
   108→- CRDs must be applied manually before `helm upgrade` (Helm limitation with kube-prometheus-stack CRDs)
   109→
   110→**Teams Integration — Power Automate (NOT legacy Office 365 connectors):**
   111→- Microsoft deprecated Office 365 Incoming Webhook connectors
   112→- Must use Power Automate workflow webhooks instead
   113→- Power Automate has a 28KB payload limit — Robusta v0.31.5+ auto-detects and disables file attachments
   114→- Webhook URL format: `https://prod-XX.westus.logic.azure.com/workflows/...`
   115→
   116→**Severity Mapping (Architecture Decision 5.3):**
   117→
   118→| Severity | Robusta Scope | Sink(s) | Response Time |
   119→|----------|--------------|---------|---------------|
   120→| Critical | `severity: HIGH` | Teams OPS + Email on-call | Immediate |
   121→| High | `severity: HIGH` | Teams OPS + Email ops | Business hours |
   122→| Medium | `severity: MEDIUM` | Email only | Next business day |
   123→| Low | `severity: LOW` / `INFO` | Grafana dashboard only | Weekly review |
   124→
   125→> **Note on severity mapping:** Robusta uses `HIGH`, `MEDIUM`, `LOW`, `INFO` internally. The architecture doc uses "Critical/High/Medium/Low". Map Critical→HIGH with scope on specific alert names (e.g., `CronJobRetryExhausted`), and High→HIGH with broader scope. Alternatively, use `labels` or `identifier` in scope to distinguish Critical from High.
   126→
   127→**Sink Routing Pattern:**
   128→- Use `scope.include` blocks on each sink to filter by severity
   129→- Use `stop: true` on higher-priority sinks to prevent duplicate notifications
   130→- Use `default: true` on the fallback email sink
   131→- Playbook-level `sinks:` directive for job-failure-specific routing
   132→
   133→### Robusta Helm Values Structure
   134→
   135→The primary configuration goes into the Robusta `generated_values.yaml` (or a custom values file). Key sections:
   136→
   137→```yaml
   138→# k8s/platform/monitoring/robusta-values.yaml (to be created)
   139→sinksConfig:
   140→  - ms_teams_sink: { ... }   # Teams OPS channel
   141→  - mail_sink: { ... }       # Critical/High email
   142→  - mail_sink: { ... }       # Medium-only email
   143→
   144→customPlaybooks:
   145→  - triggers: [on_job_failure]     # CronJob failures
   146→  - triggers: [on_pod_crash_loop]  # CrashLoopBackOff
   147→  - triggers: [on_pod_oom_killed]  # OOMKilled pods
   148→  - triggers: [on_prometheus_alert] # Custom PrometheusRule alerts
   149→
   150→enablePrometheusStack: true
   151→kube-prometheus-stack:
   152→  alertmanager:
   153→    config:
   154→      receivers:
   155→        - name: 'robusta'
   156→          webhook_configs: [...]
   157→```
   158→
   159→### Key Enrichers for CronJob Failures
   160→
   161→| Enricher | Purpose | Use When |
   162→|----------|---------|----------|
   163→| `job_info_enricher` | Job status + conditions + completion info | Every job failure |
   164→| `job_events_enricher` | `kubectl get events` for the Job object | Every job failure |
   165→| `job_pod_enricher` | Latest Pod logs + status from the Job | Every job failure |
   166→| `logs_enricher` | Pod container logs | CrashLoop, OOMKill |
   167→| `pod_events_enricher` | Pod-level events | CrashLoop, OOMKill |
   168→| `pod_graph_enricher` | Prometheus CPU/Memory graph for the pod | OOMKill, resource issues |
   169→| `alert_definition_enricher` | Shows the PromQL that fired | PrometheusRule alerts |
   170→| `graph_enricher` | Visualizes PromQL as graph in notification | PrometheusRule alerts |
   171→
   172→### PrometheusRule CRDs
   173→
   174→Create `PrometheusRule` custom resources in the `monitoring` namespace (or wherever Prometheus operator watches). The rules must have labels matching Prometheus's `ruleSelector` — when using embedded kube-prometheus-stack with `ruleSelectorNilUsesHelmValues: false`, rules from ALL namespaces are picked up.
   175→
   176→```yaml
   177→# Example: k8s/platform/monitoring/cronjob-alert-rules.yaml
   178→apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
   179→kind: PrometheusRule
   180→metadata:
   181→  name: cronjob-alerts
   182→  namespace: monitoring
   183→  labels:
   184→    prometheus: kube-prometheus
   185→    role: alert-rules
   186→spec:
   187→  groups:
   188→    - name: cronjob.rules
   189→      rules:
   190→        - alert: CronJobFailed
   191→          expr: kube_job_status_failed{namespace!="kube-system"} > 0
   192→          for: 1m
   193→          labels:
   194→            severity: warning
   195→          annotations:
   196→            summary: "CronJob {{ $labels.job_name }} failed in {{ $labels.namespace }}"
   197→```
   198→
   199→### NFR Compliance Notes
   200→
   201→- **NFR9 (2-min alert after final retry):** Use `on_job_failure` trigger (API-server based, not polling). Fires immediately when Job reaches `backoffLimit`. AlertManager `group_wait: 1s` ensures near-instant forwarding to Robusta.
   202→- **NFR10 (< 5-min detection):** Prometheus scrape interval ≤ 30s (NFR26) + AlertManager `for: 1m` evaluation + `group_wait: 1s` = ~90s worst case for metric-based alerts. API-server triggers are even faster.
   203→- **NFR24 (60s delivery):** Teams Power Automate webhook delivery is typically < 5s. Email delivery depends on SMTP relay but corporate relays typically < 30s.
   204→
   205→### Project Structure Notes
   206→
   207→**Files to create/modify:**
   208→
   209→| File | Action | Purpose |
   210→|------|--------|---------|
   211→| `k8s/platform/monitoring/robusta-values.yaml` | Create | Robusta Helm values with sinks + playbooks |
   212→| `k8s/platform/monitoring/cronjob-alert-rules.yaml` | Create | PrometheusRule CRDs for CronJob failure detection |
   213→| `terraform/modules/monitoring/main.tf` | Create | Helm release for Robusta (if using Terraform Helm provider) |
   214→| `terraform/modules/monitoring/variables.tf` | Create | Teams webhook URL, email config as variables |
   215→| `terraform/modules/monitoring/outputs.tf` | Create | Module outputs |
   216→
   217→**Alternative approach (if NOT using Terraform for Helm):**
   218→- Deploy Robusta via `helm install` directly
   219→- Store values in `k8s/platform/monitoring/robusta-values.yaml`
   220→- ArgoCD manages ongoing reconciliation
   221→
   222→**Decision for developer:** The architecture specifies `terraform/modules/monitoring/main.tf` uses `helm_release` provider to deploy Robusta. Follow this pattern unless team prefers ArgoCD-managed Helm releases.
   223→
   224→### Alignment with Existing Codebase
   225→
   226→- **k8s/platform/** directory exists (contains `security/`) — add `monitoring/` subdirectory
   227→- **No terraform/modules/ directory exists yet** — must create the full monitoring module structure
   228→- **ArgoCD ApplicationSet** at `k8s/argocd/applicationset.yaml` watches `k8s/apps/*` — the monitoring configs in `k8s/platform/monitoring/` are NOT auto-synced by this ApplicationSet (platform configs are managed separately)
   229→- **Kustomize overlays** at `k8s/overlays/dev/` and `k8s/overlays/prd/` — consider environment-specific Robusta values (different webhook URLs for DEV vs PRD)
   230→
   231→### Security Considerations
   232→
   233→- **Teams webhook URL** is a secret — store in Azure Key Vault, inject via Terraform `data.azurerm_key_vault_secret` or Kubernetes Secret
   234→- **Email SMTP credentials** — store in Key Vault, reference in Robusta values via Kubernetes Secret
   235→- **Never commit webhook URLs or SMTP passwords** to Git — use Terraform variables or sealed secrets
   236→- Robusta runner pod needs RBAC to read pod logs, events, and job status across namespaces
   237→
   238→### References
   239→
   240→- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Decision 5.3 — Alert Routing Rules]
   241→- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Epic 7 — Story 7.1]
   242→- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#FR35-FR38 — Alerting & Notification]
   243→- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#NFR9, NFR10, NFR24 — Reliability & Integration]
   244→- [Source: Robusta Docs — MS Teams Sink: docs.robusta.dev/master/configuration/sinks/ms-teams.html]
   245→- [Source: Robusta Docs — Mail Sink: docs.robusta.dev/master/configuration/sinks/mail.html]
   246→- [Source: Robusta Docs — Routing with Scopes: docs.robusta.dev/master/notification-routing/routing-with-scopes.html]
   247→- [Source: Robusta Docs — Job Failure Playbook: docs.robusta.dev/reports-docs/playbook-reference/kubernetes-examples/playbook-job-failure.html]
   248→- [Source: Robusta Docs — AlertManager Integration: docs.robusta.dev/master/configuration/alertmanager-integration/alert-manager.html]
   249→- [Source: Robusta GitHub Releases v0.33.0: github.com/robusta-dev/robusta/releases]
   250→
   251→## Dev Agent Record
   252→
   253→### Agent Model Used
   254→Claude Opus 4.6
   255→

   257→- Fixed `templatefile()` path: `../../k8s/` → `../../../k8s/` (path.module is relative to terraform/ root)
   258→- PrometheusRules embedded in Helm values (additionalPrometheusRulesMap) to avoid CRD-before-plan ordering issue with kubernetes_manifest

# Story 7.1: Configure Alert Routing to Teams and Email

Status: review

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a Platform Admin (Juliano),
I want Robusta configured to send enriched alerts to Teams and Email based on severity levels,
so that the right people are notified of issues at the right urgency.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** Robusta is deployed with playbooks and sinks configured
   **When** a job fails after all 3 retries
   **Then** an enriched alert (with pod logs, resource usage, suggested fix) is sent to Teams

2. **Given** severity-based routing is configured
   **When** a Critical severity alert fires
   **Then** it is routed to Teams OPS channel + Email to on-call (immediate response)

3. **Given** severity-based routing is configured
   **When** a High severity alert fires
   **Then** it is routed to Teams OPS channel + Email (business hours response)

4. **Given** severity-based routing is configured
   **When** a Medium severity alert fires
   **Then** it is routed to Email only (next business day review)

5. **Given** severity-based routing is configured
   **When** a Low severity alert fires
   **Then** it appears in Grafana dashboard only (weekly review)

6. **Given** an alert condition triggers
   **When** Robusta evaluates the condition
   **Then** the alert is delivered within 60 seconds of the condition trigger (NFR24)

7. **Given** a job has failed after all 3 retries
   **When** the final retry failure is detected
   **Then** an alert is sent within 2 minutes of that final failure (NFR9)

8. **Given** the alerting pipeline is operational
   **When** measured over a reporting period
   **Then** mean failure detection time is < 5 minutes across all alert types (NFR10)

## Dev Notes

### Critical Dependency: Epic 5 (Monitoring Stack)

**This story REQUIRES the monitoring stack from Epic 5 (Story 5.1) to be deployed first.** Specifically:
- Robusta with embedded kube-prometheus-stack (Prometheus + Grafana + AlertManager)
- Prometheus scraping CronJob pod metrics
- Grafana with AlertManager datasource configured

If Epic 5 is not yet implemented, the developer must either:
1. Implement Story 5.1 first (deploy LGTM stack), OR
2. Deploy a minimal Robusta installation sufficient for alerting (without full LGTM backends)

**Recommended approach:** Deploy Robusta with `enablePrometheusStack: true` as a self-contained unit. The full LGTM backends (Mimir, Loki, Tempo) can be added later in Story 5.1 without disrupting the alerting pipeline.

### Architecture Patterns and Constraints

**Robusta Version:** Use Robusta Helm chart v0.33.0+ (latest stable as of March 2026)
- Chart repo: `https://robusta-charts.storage.googleapis.com`
- **BREAKING CHANGE in v0.32.0:** Holmes AI dependency updated to 0.19.0 with breaking changes. If using Holmes, review changelog before deploying.
- CRDs must be applied manually before `helm upgrade` (Helm limitation with kube-prometheus-stack CRDs)

**Teams Integration — Power Automate (NOT legacy Office 365 connectors):**
- Microsoft deprecated Office 365 Incoming Webhook connectors
- Must use Power Automate workflow webhooks instead
- Power Automate has a 28KB payload limit — Robusta v0.31.5+ auto-detects and disables file attachments
- Webhook URL format: `https://prod-XX.westus.logic.azure.com/workflows/...`

**Severity Mapping (Architecture Decision 5.3):**

| Severity | Robusta Scope | Sink(s) | Response Time |
|----------|--------------|---------|---------------|
| Critical | `severity: HIGH` | Teams OPS + Email on-call | Immediate |
| High | `severity: HIGH` | Teams OPS + Email ops | Business hours |
| Medium | `severity: MEDIUM` | Email only | Next business day |
| Low | `severity: LOW` / `INFO` | Grafana dashboard only | Weekly review |

> **Note on severity mapping:** Robusta uses `HIGH`, `MEDIUM`, `LOW`, `INFO` internally. The architecture doc uses "Critical/High/Medium/Low". Map Critical→HIGH with scope on specific alert names (e.g., `CronJobRetryExhausted`), and High→HIGH with broader scope. Alternatively, use `labels` or `identifier` in scope to distinguish Critical from High.

**Sink Routing Pattern:**
- Use `scope.include` blocks on each sink to filter by severity
- Use `stop: true` on higher-priority sinks to prevent duplicate notifications
- Use `default: true` on the fallback email sink
- Playbook-level `sinks:` directive for job-failure-specific routing

### Robusta Helm Values Structure

The primary configuration goes into the Robusta `generated_values.yaml` (or a custom values file). Key sections:

```yaml
# k8s/platform/monitoring/robusta-values.yaml (to be created)
sinksConfig:
  - ms_teams_sink: { ... }   # Teams OPS channel
  - mail_sink: { ... }       # Critical/High email
  - mail_sink: { ... }       # Medium-only email

customPlaybooks:
  - triggers: [on_job_failure]     # CronJob failures
  - triggers: [on_pod_crash_loop]  # CrashLoopBackOff
  - triggers: [on_pod_oom_killed]  # OOMKilled pods
  - triggers: [on_prometheus_alert] # Custom PrometheusRule alerts

enablePrometheusStack: true
kube-prometheus-stack:
  alertmanager:
    config:
      receivers:
        - name: 'robusta'
          webhook_configs: [...]
```

### Key Enrichers for CronJob Failures

| Enricher | Purpose | Use When |
|----------|---------|----------|
| `job_info_enricher` | Job status + conditions + completion info | Every job failure |
| `job_events_enricher` | `kubectl get events` for the Job object | Every job failure |
| `job_pod_enricher` | Latest Pod logs + status from the Job | Every job failure |
| `logs_enricher` | Pod container logs | CrashLoop, OOMKill |
| `pod_events_enricher` | Pod-level events | CrashLoop, OOMKill |
| `pod_graph_enricher` | Prometheus CPU/Memory graph for the pod | OOMKill, resource issues |
| `alert_definition_enricher` | Shows the PromQL that fired | PrometheusRule alerts |
| `graph_enricher` | Visualizes PromQL as graph in notification | PrometheusRule alerts |

### PrometheusRule CRDs

Create `PrometheusRule` custom resources in the `monitoring` namespace (or wherever Prometheus operator watches). The rules must have labels matching Prometheus's `ruleSelector` — when using embedded kube-prometheus-stack with `ruleSelectorNilUsesHelmValues: false`, rules from ALL namespaces are picked up.

```yaml
# Example: k8s/platform/monitoring/cronjob-alert-rules.yaml
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: PrometheusRule
metadata:
  name: cronjob-alerts
  namespace: monitoring
  labels:
    prometheus: kube-prometheus
    role: alert-rules
spec:
  groups:
    - name: cronjob.rules
      rules:
        - alert: CronJobFailed
          expr: kube_job_status_failed{namespace!="kube-system"} > 0
          for: 1m
          labels:
            severity: warning
          annotations:
            summary: "CronJob {{ $labels.job_name }} failed in {{ $labels.namespace }}"
```

### NFR Compliance Notes

- **NFR9 (2-min alert after final retry):** Use `on_job_failure` trigger (API-server based, not polling). Fires immediately when Job reaches `backoffLimit`. AlertManager `group_wait: 1s` ensures near-instant forwarding to Robusta.
- **NFR10 (< 5-min detection):** Prometheus scrape interval ≤ 30s (NFR26) + AlertManager `for: 1m` evaluation + `group_wait: 1s` = ~90s worst case for metric-based alerts. API-server triggers are even faster.
- **NFR24 (60s delivery):** Teams Power Automate webhook delivery is typically < 5s. Email delivery depends on SMTP relay but corporate relays typically < 30s.

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Robusta Helm Values — Alert Routing Configuration
# Story 7.1: Configure Alert Routing to Teams and Email
#
# Architecture Decision 5.3: Severity-based routing via Robusta sinks
# NFR9:  Alert within 2 min of final retry failure
# NFR10: Mean failure detection < 5 min
# NFR24: Alert delivery within 60 seconds of condition trigger
#
# SECRETS: Teams webhook URLs and SMTP credentials are injected via
# Terraform variables → Kubernetes Secrets. Do NOT hardcode here.
# Placeholder values (PLACEHOLDER_*) are replaced by Terraform templatefile().
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Global settings
clusterName: "${cluster_name}"
isSmallCluster: false

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Sink Configuration (Tasks 1-3)
#
# Routing strategy:
#   Critical → Teams OPS + Email on-call (immediate)
#   High     → Teams OPS + Email ops (business hours)
#   Medium   → Email only (next business day)
#   Low/Info → Grafana dashboard only (weekly review)
#
# Severity mapping:
#   Robusta uses HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, INFO internally.
#   "Critical" is mapped via alert name matching (CronJobRetryExhausted,
#   KubeJobFailed with retries exhausted) on the HIGH scope.
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

sinksConfig:
  # ── Task 1: Teams OPS Channel (Critical + High) ──────────────────────
  # Power Automate webhook (NOT legacy Office 365 connector).
  # Power Automate has 28KB payload limit — Robusta v0.31.5+ auto-detects
  # and disables file attachments for Power Automate URLs.
  - ms_teams_sink:
      name: teams_ops_critical
      webhook_url: "${teams_webhook_url}"
      scope:
        include:
          - severity: HIGH
            identifier:
              - CronJobRetryExhausted
              - KubeJobFailed
              - KubePodCrashLooping
              - KubePodNotReady

  - ms_teams_sink:
      name: teams_ops_high
      webhook_url: "${teams_webhook_url}"
      scope:
        include:
          - severity: HIGH
        exclude:
          - identifier:
              - CronJobRetryExhausted
              - KubeJobFailed
              - KubePodCrashLooping
              - KubePodNotReady

  # ── Task 2: Email Sinks ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Critical → on-call email (immediate response)
  - mail_sink:
      name: email_oncall_critical
      mailto: "${smtp_url}"
      with_header: true
      scope:
        include:
          - severity: HIGH
            identifier:
              - CronJobRetryExhausted
              - KubeJobFailed
              - KubePodCrashLooping

  # High → ops team email (business hours)
  - mail_sink:
      name: email_ops_high
      mailto: "${smtp_url_ops}"
      with_header: true
      scope:
        include:
          - severity: HIGH
        exclude:
          - identifier:
              - CronJobRetryExhausted
              - KubeJobFailed
              - KubePodCrashLooping

  # Medium → ops team email only (next business day)
  - mail_sink:
      name: email_ops_medium
      mailto: "${smtp_url_ops}"
      with_header: true
      scope:
        include:
          - severity: MEDIUM

  # ── Task 3: Low severity — no sink (dashboard only) ─────────────────
  # Low/Info alerts are NOT routed to any sink. They remain in
  # AlertManager/Grafana for dashboard-only visibility.
  # No explicit sink needed — absence from all sinks = dashboard only.

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Custom Playbooks (Task 4: CronJob failure enrichment)
#
# Enrichment strategy:
#   - on_job_failure:    API-server trigger, immediate (no Prometheus needed)
#   - on_pod_crash_loop: API-server trigger, enriched with logs + events
#   - on_pod_oom_killed: API-server trigger, enriched with memory graph
#   - on_prometheus_alert: Metric-based alerts from PrometheusRule CRDs
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

customPlaybooks:
  # ── Task 4.1: Job failure with full enrichment ──────────────────────
  # Fires immediately via API-server watch (NFR9: < 2 min).
  - triggers:
      - on_job_failure: {}
    actions:
      - create_finding:
          title: "CronJob Failed"
          aggregation_key: "CronJobFailure"
          severity: HIGH
      - job_info_enricher: {}
      - job_events_enricher: {}
      - job_pod_enricher: {}
    sinks:
      - teams_ops_critical
      - email_oncall_critical

  # ── Task 4.2: CrashLoopBackOff enrichment ──────────────────────────
  - triggers:
      - on_pod_crash_loop:
          rate_limit: 900
    actions:
      - create_finding:
          title: "Pod CrashLoopBackOff"
          aggregation_key: "PodCrashLoop"
          severity: HIGH
      - logs_enricher: {}
      - pod_events_enricher: {}
    sinks:
      - teams_ops_critical
      - email_oncall_critical

  # ── Task 4.3: OOMKilled with memory graph ──────────────────────────
  - triggers:
      - on_pod_oom_killed:
          rate_limit: 900
    actions:
      - create_finding:
          title: "Pod OOMKilled"
          aggregation_key: "PodOOMKilled"
          severity: HIGH
      - pod_graph_enricher:
          resource_type: Memory
          display_limits: true
      - logs_enricher: {}
      - oom_killer_enricher: {}
    sinks:
      - teams_ops_critical
      - email_oncall_critical

  # ── Task 5.3: PrometheusRule alert enrichment ──────────────────────
  # Enriches alerts fired from the PrometheusRule CRDs (Task 5).
  - triggers:
      - on_prometheus_alert:
          alert_name: CronJobFailed
    actions:
      - logs_enricher: {}
      - job_info_enricher: {}
      - job_events_enricher: {}
      - alert_definition_enricher: {}
      - graph_enricher: {}
    sinks:
      - teams_ops_high
      - email_ops_high

  - triggers:
      - on_prometheus_alert:
          alert_name: CronJobRetryExhausted
    actions:
      - logs_enricher: {}
      - job_info_enricher: {}
      - job_events_enricher: {}
      - alert_definition_enricher: {}
      - graph_enricher: {}
    sinks:
      - teams_ops_critical
      - email_oncall_critical

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prometheus Stack (Task 6: AlertManager → Robusta integration)
#
# Deploys embedded kube-prometheus-stack with:
#   - Prometheus (metrics collection, 30s scrape interval per NFR26)
#   - AlertManager (alert routing to Robusta webhook receiver)
#   - Grafana (dashboard UI with AlertManager datasource)
#
# NOTE: This is a minimal deployment for alerting. Full LGTM backends
# (Mimir, Loki, Tempo) will be added in Epic 5 Story 5.1.
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

enablePrometheusStack: true

kube-prometheus-stack:
  alertmanager:
    config:
      receivers:
        # ── Task 6.1: Robusta webhook receiver ────────────────────────
        - name: "robusta"
          webhook_configs:
            - url: "http://robusta-runner.${robusta_namespace}.svc.cluster.local/api/alerts"
              send_resolved: true  # Task 6.3: resolved event tracking

        - name: "default-receiver"
          webhook_configs: []

      route:
        receiver: "default-receiver"
        routes:
          - receiver: "robusta"
            group_by: ["..."]
            # ── Task 6.2: Fast alert delivery (NFR24: < 60s) ─────────
            group_wait: "1s"
            group_interval: "1s"
            repeat_interval: "4h"
            matchers:
              - severity =~ ".*"
            continue: true  # Allow other receivers to process too

  prometheus:
    prometheusSpec:
      # Pick up PrometheusRules from any namespace
      ruleNamespaceSelector: {}
      ruleSelector: {}
      ruleSelectorNilUsesHelmValues: false
      # NFR26: scraping interval <= 30 seconds
      scrapeInterval: "30s"
      # Short retention — long-term storage via Mimir in Epic 5
      retention: "15d"

  grafana:
    # Grafana deployed as part of kube-prometheus-stack
    # Task 3.2: AlertManager datasource is auto-configured
    adminPassword: "${grafana_admin_password}"
    sidecar:
      dashboards:
        enabled: true
        searchNamespace: ALL

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # PrometheusRule CRDs (Task 5)
  # Embedded in Helm values to avoid CRD-before-plan ordering issues.
  # These rules fire alerts that Robusta enriches via customPlaybooks.
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  additionalPrometheusRulesMap:
    # ── Task 5.1: CronJobFailed ──────────────────────────────────────
    cronjob-failure-rules:
      groups:
        - name: cronjob.failure.rules
          rules:
            - alert: CronJobFailed
              expr: kube_job_status_failed{namespace!~"kube-system|argocd|monitoring"} > 0
              for: 1m
              labels:
                severity: warning
              annotations:
                summary: "CronJob {{ $labels.job_name }} failed in {{ $labels.namespace }}"
                description: "Job {{ $labels.job_name }} in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }} has failed. Check pod logs and events."
                runbook_url: "https://docs.internal/runbooks/cronjob-failure"

    # ── Task 5.2: CronJobRetryExhausted ──────────────────────────────
    cronjob-retry-rules:
      groups:
        - name: cronjob.retry.rules
          rules:
            - alert: CronJobRetryExhausted
              expr: kube_job_status_failed{namespace!~"kube-system|argocd|monitoring"} >= 3
              for: 30s
              labels:
                severity: critical
              annotations:
                summary: "CronJob {{ $labels.job_name }} exhausted all retries in {{ $labels.namespace }}"
                description: "Job {{ $labels.job_name }} in {{ $labels.namespace }} failed 3+ times. All retries exhausted. Immediate investigation per NFR9."
                runbook_url: "https://docs.internal/runbooks/cronjob-retry-exhausted"

    # ── CronJob scheduling health ────────────────────────────────────
    cronjob-scheduling-rules:
      groups:
        - name: cronjob.scheduling.rules
          rules:
            - alert: CronJobNotScheduled
              expr: time() - kube_cronjob_next_schedule_time{namespace!~"kube-system|argocd|monitoring"} > 3600
              for: 10m
              labels:
                severity: warning
              annotations:
                summary: "CronJob {{ $labels.cronjob }} overdue in {{ $labels.namespace }}"
                description: "CronJob {{ $labels.cronjob }} in {{ $labels.namespace }} is 1h+ past expected schedule."
            - alert: CronJobLastRunTooOld
              expr: time() - kube_cronjob_status_last_schedule_time{namespace!~"kube-system|argocd|monitoring"} > 86400
              for: 1h
              labels:
                severity: warning
              annotations:
                summary: "CronJob {{ $labels.cronjob }} hasn't run in 24h in {{ $labels.namespace }}"
                description: "CronJob {{ $labels.cronjob }} in {{ $labels.namespace }} last ran 24h+ ago."

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PrometheusRule — CronJob Failure Detection
# Story 7.1: Configure Alert Routing to Teams and Email
#
# Task 5: Create PrometheusRule for CronJob failure detection
# NFR9:  Alert within 2 min of final retry failure
# NFR10: Mean failure detection < 5 min
# NFR26: Prometheus scraping interval <= 30 seconds
#
# Applied to the monitoring namespace. With ruleSelectorNilUsesHelmValues
# set to false in Prometheus config, rules from ALL namespaces are picked up.
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: PrometheusRule
metadata:
  name: cronjob-alerts
  namespace: monitoring
  labels:
    prometheus: kube-prometheus
    role: alert-rules
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: python-governance
spec:
  groups:
    # ── Task 5.1: CronJobFailed ────────────────────────────────────────
    # Detects any Job failure across governed namespaces.
    # Severity: warning (routed to Teams OPS + Email ops via Robusta).
    - name: cronjob.failure.rules
      rules:
        - alert: CronJobFailed
          expr: |
            kube_job_status_failed{namespace!~"kube-system|argocd|monitoring"} > 0
          for: 1m
          labels:
            severity: warning
          annotations:
            summary: "CronJob {{ $labels.job_name }} failed in {{ $labels.namespace }}"
            description: >-
              Job {{ $labels.job_name }} in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }}
              has failed. Check pod logs and events for root cause.
            runbook_url: "https://docs.internal/runbooks/cronjob-failure"

    # ── Task 5.2: CronJobRetryExhausted ────────────────────────────────
    # Detects when a Job has reached its backoffLimit (3 retries exhausted).
    # Severity: critical (routed to Teams OPS + Email on-call via Robusta).
    # Uses kube_job_status_failed >= backoffLimit to detect exhaustion.
    - name: cronjob.retry.rules
      rules:
        - alert: CronJobRetryExhausted
          expr: |
            kube_job_status_failed{namespace!~"kube-system|argocd|monitoring"} >= 3
          for: 30s
          labels:
            severity: critical
          annotations:
            summary: "CronJob {{ $labels.job_name }} exhausted all retries in {{ $labels.namespace }}"
            description: >-
              Job {{ $labels.job_name }} in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }}
              has failed 3+ times (backoffLimit reached). All automatic retries
              exhausted. Immediate investigation required per NFR9.
            runbook_url: "https://docs.internal/runbooks/cronjob-retry-exhausted"

    # ── CronJob scheduling health ──────────────────────────────────────
    # Detects CronJobs that have not run within expected schedule window.
    - name: cronjob.scheduling.rules
      rules:
        - alert: CronJobNotScheduled
          expr: |
            time() - kube_cronjob_next_schedule_time{namespace!~"kube-system|argocd|monitoring"} > 3600
          for: 10m
          labels:
            severity: warning
          annotations:
            summary: "CronJob {{ $labels.cronjob }} overdue in {{ $labels.namespace }}"
            description: >-
              CronJob {{ $labels.cronjob }} in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }}
              has not been scheduled for over 1 hour past its next expected run.
              May indicate suspended CronJob or scheduling issue.

        - alert: CronJobLastRunTooOld
          expr: |
            time() - kube_cronjob_status_last_schedule_time{namespace!~"kube-system|argocd|monitoring"} > 86400
          for: 1h
          labels:
            severity: warning
          annotations:
            summary: "CronJob {{ $labels.cronjob }} hasn't run in 24h in {{ $labels.namespace }}"
            description: >-
              CronJob {{ $labels.cronjob }} in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }}
              last ran more than 24 hours ago. Verify schedule and CronJob status.

1→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
     2→# Monitoring Module (Story 7.1)
     3→# Deploys Robusta with embedded kube-prometheus-stack via Helm provider.
     4→#
     5→# Architecture Decision 5.3: Severity-based alert routing via Robusta sinks
     6→# Architecture: terraform/modules/monitoring/main.tf uses helm_release
     7→#
     8→# Robusta provides:
     9→#   - Alert enrichment (pod logs, resource usage, suggested fixes)
    10→#   - Sink routing (Teams, Email) with severity-based scoping
    11→#   - Custom playbooks for CronJob failure detection
    12→#   - Embedded Prometheus + Grafana + AlertManager
    13→#
    14→# NOTE: This is a focused deployment for alerting. Full LGTM backends
    15→# (Mimir remote-write, Loki, Tempo) will be added in Epic 5 Story 5.1.
    16→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→
    18→terraform {
    19→  required_providers {
    20→    helm = {
    21→      source  = "hashicorp/helm"
    22→      version = "~> 2.0"
    23→    }
    24→    kubernetes = {
    25→      source  = "hashicorp/kubernetes"
    26→      version = "~> 2.35"
    27→    }
    28→  }
    29→}
    30→
    31→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32→# Robusta Helm Release
    33→#
    34→# Chart: robusta/robusta from robusta-charts.storage.googleapis.com
    35→# Includes embedded kube-prometheus-stack (Prometheus + Grafana + AlertManager)
    36→#
    37→# CRD handling: kube-prometheus-stack CRDs are bundled. Helm cannot
    38→# auto-update CRDs on upgrade. Apply CRDs manually before helm upgrade:
    39→#   kubectl apply --server-side -f <prometheus-operator-crd-url>
    40→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    41→
    42→resource "helm_release" "robusta" {
    43→  name             = "robusta"
    44→  repository       = "https://robusta-charts.storage.googleapis.com"
    45→  chart            = "robusta"
    46→  version          = var.robusta_chart_version
    47→  namespace        = var.robusta_namespace
    48→  create_namespace = true
    49→  timeout          = var.robusta_helm_timeout
    50→  wait             = true
    51→  atomic           = true
    52→
    53→  # Render the values template with environment-specific variables.
    54→  # The template file uses ${var} interpolation for secrets.
    55→  values = [
    56→    templatefile("${path.module}/../../../k8s/platform/monitoring/robusta-values.yaml", {
    57→      cluster_name           = var.cluster_name
    58→      teams_webhook_url      = var.teams_webhook_url
    59→      smtp_url               = var.smtp_url
    60→      smtp_url_ops           = var.smtp_url_ops
    61→      robusta_namespace      = var.robusta_namespace
    62→      grafana_admin_password = var.grafana_admin_password
    63→    })
    64→  ]
    65→

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Monitoring Module (Story 7.1)
# Deploys Robusta with embedded kube-prometheus-stack via Helm provider.
#
# Architecture Decision 5.3: Severity-based alert routing via Robusta sinks
# Architecture: terraform/modules/monitoring/main.tf uses helm_release
#
# Robusta provides:
#   - Alert enrichment (pod logs, resource usage, suggested fixes)
#   - Sink routing (Teams, Email) with severity-based scoping
#   - Custom playbooks for CronJob failure detection
#   - Embedded Prometheus + Grafana + AlertManager
#
# NOTE: This is a focused deployment for alerting. Full LGTM backends
# (Mimir remote-write, Loki, Tempo) will be added in Epic 5 Story 5.1.
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

terraform {
  required_providers {
    helm = {
      source  = "hashicorp/helm"
      version = "~> 2.0"
    }
    kubernetes = {
      source  = "hashicorp/kubernetes"
      version = "~> 2.35"
    }
  }
}

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Robusta Helm Release
#
# Chart: robusta/robusta from robusta-charts.storage.googleapis.com
# Includes embedded kube-prometheus-stack (Prometheus + Grafana + AlertManager)
#
# CRD handling: kube-prometheus-stack CRDs are bundled. Helm cannot
# auto-update CRDs on upgrade. Apply CRDs manually before helm upgrade:
#   kubectl apply --server-side -f <prometheus-operator-crd-url>
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

resource "helm_release" "robusta" {
  name             = "robusta"
  repository       = "https://robusta-charts.storage.googleapis.com"
  chart            = "robusta"
  version          = var.robusta_chart_version
  namespace        = var.robusta_namespace
  create_namespace = true
  timeout          = var.robusta_helm_timeout
  wait             = true
  atomic           = true

  # Render the values template with environment-specific variables.
  # The template file uses ${var} interpolation for secrets.
  values = [
    templatefile("${path.module}/../../../k8s/platform/monitoring/robusta-values.yaml", {
      cluster_name           = var.cluster_name
      teams_webhook_url      = var.teams_webhook_url
      smtp_url               = var.smtp_url
      smtp_url_ops           = var.smtp_url_ops
      robusta_namespace      = var.robusta_namespace
      grafana_admin_password = var.grafana_admin_password
    })
  ]

  # Environment label for resource identification
  set {
    name  = "global.additionalLabels.environment"
    value = var.environment
  }

  set {
    name  = "global.additionalLabels.app\\.kubernetes\\.io/part-of"
    value = "python-governance"
  }
}

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PrometheusRule CRDs (Task 5)
# Embedded in Robusta Helm values via kube-prometheus-stack's
# additionalPrometheusRulesMap. This avoids CRD-before-plan ordering
# issues with kubernetes_manifest resources.
#
# Standalone YAML reference: k8s/platform/monitoring/cronjob-alert-rules.yaml
# (for kubectl apply if deploying outside Terraform)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

module "networking" {
  source = "./modules/networking"

  environment                   = var.environment
  location                      = var.location
  resource_group_name           = var.resource_group_name
  vnet_address_space            = var.vnet_address_space
  aks_system_subnet_cidr        = var.aks_system_subnet_cidr
  aks_user_subnet_cidr          = var.aks_user_subnet_cidr
  private_endpoints_subnet_cidr = var.private_endpoints_subnet_cidr
  sap_vnet_id                   = var.sap_vnet_id
  sap_vnet_resource_group       = var.sap_vnet_resource_group
  sap_dns_zone_name             = var.sap_dns_zone_name
  sap_dns_records               = var.sap_dns_records
  sap_dns_record_ttl            = var.sap_dns_record_ttl
  sap_allow_gateway_transit     = var.sap_allow_gateway_transit
  sap_use_remote_gateways       = var.sap_use_remote_gateways
  tags                          = var.tags

  providers = {
    azurerm     = azurerm
    azurerm.sap = azurerm.sap
  }
}

module "aks" {
  source = "./modules/aks"

  environment         = var.environment
  location            = var.location
  resource_group_name = var.resource_group_name

  # AKS cluster configuration
  kubernetes_version         = var.kubernetes_version
  aks_sku_tier               = var.aks_sku_tier
  aks_admin_group_object_ids = var.aks_admin_group_object_ids

  # Networking — from Story 1.1 module outputs
  aks_system_subnet_id = module.networking.aks_system_subnet_id
  aks_user_subnet_id   = module.networking.aks_user_subnet_id

  # System node pool
  system_node_pool_vm_size    = var.system_node_pool_vm_size
  system_node_pool_node_count = var.system_node_pool_node_count

  # User (workload) node pool
  user_node_pool_vm_size   = var.user_node_pool_vm_size
  user_node_pool_min_count = var.user_node_pool_min_count
  user_node_pool_max_count = var.user_node_pool_max_count

  # ACR
  acr_name = var.acr_name
  acr_sku  = var.acr_sku

  # Service CIDR (must not overlap with VNet 10.0.0.0/16 DEV or 10.1.0.0/16 PRD)
  service_cidr   = var.service_cidr
  dns_service_ip = var.dns_service_ip

  tags = var.tags
}

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Namespace Isolation (Story 1.3)
# Depends on AKS cluster for Kubernetes provider connectivity.
#
# IMPORTANT: The kubernetes provider references module.aks outputs.
# On first deploy, apply networking and AKS first:
#   terraform apply -target=module.networking -target=module.aks
# Then apply the full configuration (including namespaces):
#   terraform apply
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

module "namespaces" {
  source = "./modules/namespaces"

  environment = var.environment
  namespaces  = var.namespaces

  # RBAC — AD Group Object IDs
  ad_group_dev_object_id    = var.ad_group_dev_object_id
  ad_group_review_object_id = var.ad_group_review_object_id
  ad_group_ops_object_id    = var.ad_group_ops_object_id
  ad_group_admin_object_id  = var.ad_group_admin_object_id

  # Network Policy — SAP egress
  default_sap_egress_cidrs = var.default_sap_egress_cidrs
  default_sap_egress_ports = var.default_sap_egress_ports

  depends_on = [module.aks]
}

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Secure Credential Management (Story 3.1)
# Key Vault + per-namespace UAMI + Workload Identity federation.
# Depends on AKS for OIDC issuer URL and networking for subnet ID.
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

module "security" {
  source = "./modules/security"

  environment         = var.environment
  location            = var.location
  resource_group_name = var.resource_group_name

  # Namespace list — derived from keys of the namespaces map
  namespaces = keys(var.namespaces)

  # AKS integration — OIDC issuer for Workload Identity federation
  aks_oidc_issuer_url = module.aks.oidc_issuer_url

  # Network ACL — allow AKS user subnet to access Key Vault
  aks_subnet_id = module.networking.aks_user_subnet_id

  # Key Vault configuration
  key_vault_sku                        = var.key_vault_sku
  key_vault_soft_delete_retention_days = var.key_vault_soft_delete_retention_days

  # Audit configuration (Story 3.3)
  audit_log_retention_days = var.audit_log_retention_days

  tags = var.tags

  depends_on = [module.aks]
}

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CI / Azure DevOps Configuration (Story 4.1)
# Azure DevOps project, Git repository, and branch policies.
# Independent of AKS — uses separate azuredevops provider.
#
# IMPORTANT: Requires AZDO_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN environment variable set
# with Code (Read, Create & Manage) and Build (Read & Manage) permissions.
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

module "ci" {
  source = "./modules/ci"

  project_name       = var.azuredevops_project_name
  repository_name    = var.azuredevops_repository_name
  organization_url   = var.azuredevops_org_url
  environment        = var.environment
  min_reviewer_count = var.min_reviewer_count

  # Story 4.3: ACR service connection for image push pipeline
  acr_name_short          = var.acr_name
  acr_resource_group_name = var.resource_group_name
  subscription_id         = var.subscription_id
  subscription_name       = var.azure_subscription_name
  tenant_id               = var.azure_tenant_id
}

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ArgoCD GitOps Deployment (Story 4.4)
# Deploys ArgoCD via Helm with ApplicationSets for auto-discovery of
# namespace directories in k8s/apps/<namespace>/<cronjob>/.
# Depends on AKS for Kubernetes/Helm provider connectivity.
#
# Key NFRs:
#   NFR12 — Self-heal drift revert within 3 minutes (reconciliation: 180s)
#   NFR23 — Sync latency < 5 minutes from merge-to-main
#
# IMPORTANT: The helm provider references module.aks outputs.
# AKS must be deployed first (same as kubernetes provider).
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Monitoring / Alerting (Story 7.1)
# Deploys Robusta with embedded kube-prometheus-stack for severity-based
# alert routing to Teams and Email.
# Depends on AKS for Helm provider connectivity.
#
# Key NFRs:
#   NFR9  — Alert within 2 min of final retry failure
#   NFR10 — Mean failure detection < 5 min
#   NFR24 — Alert delivery within 60 seconds
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

module "monitoring" {
  source = "./modules/monitoring"

  environment           = var.environment
  cluster_name          = module.aks.cluster_name
  robusta_namespace     = var.robusta_namespace
  robusta_chart_version = var.robusta_chart_version
  robusta_helm_timeout  = var.robusta_helm_timeout

  # Alert sink credentials (sensitive — never commit values)
  teams_webhook_url      = var.teams_webhook_url
  smtp_url               = var.smtp_url
  smtp_url_ops           = var.smtp_url_ops
  grafana_admin_password = var.grafana_admin_password

  tags = var.tags

  depends_on = [module.aks]
}

module "argocd" {
  source = "./modules/argocd"

  environment          = var.environment
  argocd_namespace     = var.argocd_namespace
  argocd_chart_version = var.argocd_chart_version
  argocd_helm_timeout  = var.argocd_helm_timeout
  git_repo_url         = var.argocd_git_repo_url
  git_target_revision  = var.argocd_git_target_revision

  # Drift detection — NFR12: revert within 3 minutes
  reconciliation_timeout_seconds = var.argocd_reconciliation_timeout_seconds

  # PRD manual approval gate — Decision 4.3
  prd_sync_window_enabled  = var.argocd_prd_sync_window_enabled
  prd_sync_window_schedule = var.argocd_prd_sync_window_schedule

  tags = var.tags

  depends_on = [module.aks]
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Story 7.1 — End-to-End Alert Routing Validation
# Task 7: Validate alert delivery across all severity levels.
#
# Prerequisites:
#   - kubectl configured and pointing to the target AKS cluster
#   - Robusta deployed in 'monitoring' namespace
#   - PrometheusRule CRDs applied
#
# Usage:
#   ./terraform/scripts/alert-routing-test.sh [namespace]
#
# Default namespace: logistica (pilot namespace)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

set -euo pipefail

NAMESPACE="${1:-logistica}"
TEST_JOB_NAME="alert-routing-test-$(date +%s)"
MONITORING_NS="monitoring"
PASS=0
FAIL=0

echo "=========================================="
echo "Story 7.1: Alert Routing Validation"
echo "=========================================="
echo "Cluster:   $(kubectl config current-context)"
echo "Namespace: ${NAMESPACE}"
echo "Test Job:  ${TEST_JOB_NAME}"
echo ""

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pre-flight: Verify Robusta is running
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "── Pre-flight checks ──────────────────────"

if kubectl get deployment robusta-runner -n "${MONITORING_NS}" &>/dev/null; then
  ROBUSTA_READY=$(kubectl get deployment robusta-runner -n "${MONITORING_NS}" -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}')
  if [[ "${ROBUSTA_READY}" -ge 1 ]]; then
    echo "✅ Robusta runner is ready (${ROBUSTA_READY} replicas)"
    ((PASS++))
  else
    echo "❌ Robusta runner has 0 ready replicas"
    ((FAIL++))
  fi
else
  echo "❌ Robusta runner deployment not found in ${MONITORING_NS}"
  ((FAIL++))
fi

# Verify Prometheus is running
if kubectl get statefulset prometheus-robusta-kube-prometheus-st-prometheus -n "${MONITORING_NS}" &>/dev/null; then
  echo "✅ Prometheus StatefulSet found"
  ((PASS++))
else
  echo "❌ Prometheus not found in ${MONITORING_NS}"
  ((FAIL++))
fi

# Verify AlertManager is running
if kubectl get statefulset alertmanager-robusta-kube-prometheus-st-alertmanager -n "${MONITORING_NS}" &>/dev/null; then
  echo "✅ AlertManager StatefulSet found"
  ((PASS++))
else
  echo "❌ AlertManager not found in ${MONITORING_NS}"
  ((FAIL++))
fi

# Verify PrometheusRule CRD exists
if kubectl get prometheusrule cronjob-alerts -n "${MONITORING_NS}" &>/dev/null; then
  echo "✅ PrometheusRule 'cronjob-alerts' found"
  ((PASS++))
else
  echo "❌ PrometheusRule 'cronjob-alerts' not found"
  ((FAIL++))
fi

echo ""

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Task 7.1: Deploy a test CronJob that intentionally fails
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "── Task 7.1: Deploy failing test job ──────"

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: ${TEST_JOB_NAME}
  namespace: ${NAMESPACE}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: python-governance
    test: alert-routing
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  activeDeadlineSeconds: 30
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        test: alert-routing
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
        - name: fail-test
          image: busybox:1.36
          command: ["sh", "-c", "echo 'Alert routing test — intentional failure'; exit 1"]
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "50m"
              memory: "32Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "100m"
              memory: "64Mi"
EOF

echo "✅ Test job '${TEST_JOB_NAME}' created"
((PASS++))

# Wait for job to fail
echo "   Waiting for job to fail (max 60s)..."
START_TIME=$(date +%s)
JOB_FAILED=false

for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get job "${TEST_JOB_NAME}" -n "${NAMESPACE}" -o jsonpath='{.status.failed}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  if [[ "${STATUS}" -ge 1 ]]; then
    ELAPSED=$(( $(date +%s) - START_TIME ))
    echo "✅ Job failed after ${ELAPSED}s (expected)"
    JOB_FAILED=true
    ((PASS++))
    break
  fi
  sleep 1
done

if [[ "${JOB_FAILED}" == "false" ]]; then
  echo "❌ Job did not fail within 60 seconds"
  ((FAIL++))
fi

echo ""

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Task 7.2 / 7.3: Check alert delivery timing
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "── Task 7.2/7.3: Alert delivery timing ────"
echo "   NOTE: Teams and Email delivery must be verified manually."
echo "   Check the following within 60 seconds of job failure:"
echo "   - Teams OPS channel for enriched alert notification"
echo "   - On-call email inbox for critical alert email"
echo ""
echo "   Verify alert content includes:"
echo "   - Pod logs from the failed container"
echo "   - Resource usage information"
echo "   - Suggested fix or runbook link"
echo ""

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Task 7.5: Verify Grafana AlertManager shows alerts
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "── Task 7.5: Grafana dashboard verification ─"

# Check if AlertManager has received the alert
ALERTMANAGER_ALERTS=$(kubectl exec -n "${MONITORING_NS}" \
  statefulset/alertmanager-robusta-kube-prometheus-st-alertmanager -- \
  wget -qO- "http://localhost:9093/api/v2/alerts?filter=job_name%3D${TEST_JOB_NAME}" 2>/dev/null || echo "[]")

if [[ "${ALERTMANAGER_ALERTS}" != "[]" ]] && [[ -n "${ALERTMANAGER_ALERTS}" ]]; then
  echo "✅ AlertManager received alert for test job"
  ((PASS++))
else
  echo "⚠️  AlertManager alert not yet visible (may take up to 90s for metric-based alerts)"
  echo "   The on_job_failure Robusta trigger fires independently via API-server watch."
fi

echo ""

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Cleanup
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "── Cleanup ────────────────────────────────"
kubectl delete job "${TEST_JOB_NAME}" -n "${NAMESPACE}" --ignore-not-found
echo "✅ Test job cleaned up"

echo ""
echo "=========================================="
echo "Results: ${PASS} passed, ${FAIL} failed"
echo "=========================================="

if [[ ${FAIL} -gt 0 ]]; then
  echo "⚠️  Some checks failed. Review output above."
  exit 1
fi

echo "✅ All automated checks passed."
echo ""
echo "Manual verification required:"
echo "  1. Confirm Teams notification arrived within 60s (NFR24)"
echo "  2. Confirm Email notification arrived within 60s (NFR24)"
echo "  3. Confirm enrichment includes pod logs + resource usage (AC #1)"
echo "  4. Open Grafana → Alerts → verify low-severity alerts are dashboard-only (AC #5)"

# Architecture Decision Document

_This document builds collaboratively through step-by-step discovery. Sections are appended as we work through each architectural decision together._

## Project Context Analysis

### Requirements Overview

**Functional Requirements:**
47 FRs across 9 capability groups. The architecture must support two distinct planes:
1. **Developer Plane** — boilerplate template, code push, PR workflow, credential references (FR1-6, FR7-13)
2. **Platform Plane** — build pipeline, deployment, scheduling, monitoring, cost tracking, alerting, access control, infrastructure provisioning (FR14-47)

These planes interact at well-defined boundaries: the PR merge triggers the build pipeline, the boilerplate template defines the contract between developer code and platform services, and namespace isolation separates business areas.

**Non-Functional Requirements:**
26 NFRs that impose specific architectural constraints:
- **Security (NFR1-7):** Zero-trust credential model, namespace network isolation, RBAC verification, container vulnerability scanning, SSO-only access
- **Reliability (NFR8-13):** 99.5% execution availability, auto-retry with alerting, no SPOF, ArgoCD drift self-heal, secret rotation resilience
- **Scalability (NFR14-17):** 5→60+ CronJobs growth path, node autoscaling 3-10, 20 concurrent jobs per namespace
- **Performance (NFR18-21):** <60s pod startup (cached), <3s dashboard load, <10min CI pipeline, <5s log queries
- **Integration (NFR22-26):** SAP connectivity verification, <5min ArgoCD sync, <60s alert delivery, 99% CI trigger reliability, 30s metrics scrape interval

**Scale & Complexity:**

- Primary domain: Infrastructure / Platform Engineering
- Complexity level: High
- Estimated architectural components: 12-15 (AKS cluster, ArgoCD, CI pipelines, ACR, Key Vault + CSI driver, Robusta (Prometheus + Grafana + AlertManager + alert enrichment), LGTM backends (Mimir, Loki, Tempo), Teams alerting via Robusta sinks, namespace provisioning, boilerplate template, base Docker images, network policies, RBAC configuration)

### Technical Constraints & Dependencies

- **Azure-only cloud** — corporate standard, no multi-cloud consideration needed
